name: Mountain Pose
description: The foundation of all standing poses. Feet together, spine elongated, arms at sides. Establishes grounding and body awareness.

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your left knee — stand tall
angles.correctionHigh: Soften your left knee slightly to avoid locking

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your right knee — stand tall
angles.correctionHigh: Soften your right knee slightly to avoid locking

angles.correctionLow: Extend your left hip — lengthen through the torso
angles.correctionHigh: Draw your left hip slightly forward

angles.correctionLow: Extend your right hip — lengthen through the torso
angles.correctionHigh: Draw your right hip slightly forward

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your left arm — let it hang naturally
angles.correctionHigh: Relax your left elbow — avoid over-extension

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your right arm — let it hang naturally
angles.correctionHigh: Relax your right elbow — avoid over-extension

name: Warrior I
description: A powerful standing pose that builds strength in the legs and opens the chest. Front knee bent at 90°, arms overhead.

angles.correctionLow: Bend your front knee deeper toward 90°
angles.correctionHigh: Bend your front knee more — aim for 90°

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your back leg — press through the heel
angles.correctionHigh: Extend your back leg fully

angles.correctionLow: Deepen your front hip crease
angles.correctionHigh: Sink lower — bend into the front hip

angles.correctionLow: Raise your arms higher — reach overhead
angles.correctionHigh: Keep arms actively lifted

angles.correctionLow: Raise your arms higher — reach overhead
angles.correctionHigh: Keep arms actively lifted

name: Warrior II
description: Arms extended parallel to the floor, gaze over the front hand. Cultivates stamina and opens the hips and chest.

angles.correctionLow: Bend your front knee deeper — stack over ankle
angles.correctionHigh: Bend your front knee more — aim for 90°

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your back leg — energize through the foot
angles.correctionHigh: Fully extend your back leg

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your left arm — extend through the fingertips
angles.correctionHigh: Straighten your left arm fully

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your right arm — extend through the fingertips
angles.correctionHigh: Straighten your right arm fully

name: Tree Pose
description: Single-leg balance with the raised foot pressed into the inner thigh. Improves concentration and strengthens the standing leg.

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your standing leg
angles.correctionHigh: Avoid locking the standing knee

angles.correctionLow: Bend the raised knee more — press foot into inner thigh
angles.correctionHigh: Bring the raised foot higher on the standing leg

angles.correctionLow: Stand upright — extend through your standing hip
angles.correctionHigh: Lengthen the standing side

name: Chair Pose
description: A seated squat position with arms raised. Builds heat, strengthens the thighs and glutes, and challenges endurance.

angles.correctionLow: Sit deeper into the chair — bend knees more
angles.correctionHigh: Bend your knees more — lower the hips

angles.correctionLow: Sit deeper into the chair — bend knees more
angles.correctionHigh: Bend your knees more — lower the hips

angles.correctionLow: Sit lower — deepen the hip bend
angles.correctionHigh: Bend more at the hips to lower the seat

angles.correctionLow: Sit lower — deepen the hip bend
angles.correctionHigh: Bend more at the hips to lower the seat

angles.correctionLow: Reach your arms higher — lift through the fingertips
angles.correctionHigh: Keep arms actively lifted

angles.correctionLow: Reach your arms higher — lift through the fingertips
angles.correctionHigh: Keep arms actively lifted

name: Triangle Pose
description: Legs wide, one hand reaching to the floor and the other to the sky. Stretches the hamstrings and opens the chest.

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your front leg — micro-bend only
angles.correctionHigh: Soften the front knee slightly

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your back leg — press the outer foot down
angles.correctionHigh: Fully extend the back leg

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your top arm — reach for the ceiling
angles.correctionHigh: Fully extend your arm upward

angles.correctionLow: Straighten your bottom arm — reach toward the floor
angles.correctionHigh: Fully extend your arm downward

angles.correctionLow: Hinge more at the hip — deepen the lateral bend
angles.correctionHigh: Lean further into the side stretch
